$comment: PE/COFF EXECUTABLE (PE32+) for x86_64 Windows. Second PE schema (plan 14 LK2 cycle 2, 2026-05-30). Consumed by `src/link/format/pe.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine. Differs from `pe64-x86_64-windows.format.json` (the .obj schema) in: `pe.type = exec`; sections carry `virtualAddress` (interpreted as RVA ΓÇõ offset from ImageBase); top-level `entryPoint` names the entry symbol (resolved against AssembledModule at link time); new `optionalHeader` block populated with IMAGE_OPTIONAL_HEADER64 fields. ImageBase = 0x140000000 (Windows x86_64 .exe convention per the MSVC linker default). Subsystem = IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_WINDOWS_CUI (3) ΓÇõ console subsystem; GUI exe would set 2. SectionAlignment = 0x1000 (4 KB page); FileAlignment = 0x200 (512 bytes, MSVC default).
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit Windows is LLP64 (int 32, long 32, long long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I32 here, the literal ladder + descriptor signatureByDataModel resolve their LLP64 arms. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: C bit-field allocation is OS/format-determined (the x86_64 CPU target serves BOTH this PE format and ELF-SysV), so the strategy lives here, not on the target. MSVC's x64 ABI starts a NEW allocation unit ΓÇõ aligned to the field's declared-type natural alignment ΓÇõ whenever an adjacent bit-field's declared-type SIZE differs from the current unit's type size (it never packs different-sized declared types into one unit), and the struct size covers the last unit's full declared-type width. Byte-ABI-exact to cl.exe (verified by the conformance witness): e.g. `{int a:1; char b:1;}` is sizeof 8 with b@byte4, vs gnu_packed's 4 with b@bit1.
$entryPointComment: Empty entryPoint defaults the walker to module.functions[0] (cycle-2 convention ΓÇõ same as LK1 ELF ET_EXEC). Real-name resolution closes with D-LK1-1 (HIRΓÁÈLIR symbol-name thread). D-LK10-ENTRY Slice C (CLOSED 2026-06-02, commit 1a0b414): the linker prepends a synthetic `_start` trampoline as module.functions[0]; the schema's empty entryPoint resolves to functions[0] = the trampoline, which then calls the USER fn (the post-prepend functions[1+]). See plan 14 ┬º2.13 ΓÇõ Stage 1 closed.
$externCallDispatchComment: PE resolves imports through the IAT: the linker points each extern symbol's VA at an .idata POINTER slot, and the call site dereferences it (x86_64 FF 15 disp32 = call [RIP+disp32]). D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH.
$processExitComment:
D-LK10-ENTRY Slice B (plan 14 ┬º2.13): the Windows runnable-binary spine. Trampoline emitter issues `call qword ptr [iat_ExitProcess]` to kernel32.dll's ExitProcess. The linker injects a synthetic `ExternImport{kernel32.dll, ExitProcess}` into module.externImports; the existing IAT writer (LK6 cycle 2a) emits the IAT slot; the `call_indirect_via_extern` LIR opcode (Slice A) emits FF 15 disp32 with a REL32 reloc on the disp32 patch site targeting the synthetic import's SymbolId.

STDIO-FLUSH-AT-EXIT (2026-06-09) ΓÇõ WHY PE KEEPS ExitProcess (NOT msvcrt `exit`): the user mandate is 'terminate through a stdio-flushing libc-level primitive on every hosted format'. On elf/macho that meant switching from a raw exit syscall to libc `exit(3)`. On Windows, ExitProcess ALREADY flushes the msvcrt stdio buffers and is the canonical process-termination primitive: when ExitProcess runs, the loader sends DLL_PROCESS_DETACH to every loaded DLL in reverse-load order, and msvcrt.dll's detach handler flushes + closes its CRT streams. The `puts("hello")`-then-return corpus (examples/c-subset/shipped_include_puts, hello_puts) EMPIRICALLY prints 'hello
' on Windows under captured-pipe stdout ΓÇõ proving the buffered bytes reach stdout via this path (the 
 vs 
 is msvcrt text-mode CRLF translation, confirming the bytes flow through msvcrt's FILE layer). Switching PE to call msvcrt `exit` would be a regression risk for ZERO benefit (msvcrt `exit` ultimately calls the same kernel32 process-exit after the SAME stream cleanup) and would couple the entry spine to a specific CRT export. So the 'uniform libc exit' is realized PER FORMAT: elfΓÁÈlibc `exit`, machoΓÁÈlibSystem `exit` (symbol `_exit`), peΓÁÈkernel32 `ExitProcess` (which flushes via msvcrt DLL-detach). All three deliver buffered stdout before the process dies; the mechanism is config-driven per format, with NO format identity branch in the shared trampoline.
$entryCallingConventionComment: MS x64 calling convention ΓÇõ status (user fn return value in rax) is moved into argGprs[0] = rcx before the indirect ExitProcess call. Read via `target.callingConventionByName("ms_x64")` at trampoline-build time.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: D-LK2-RODATA (`.rdata`) + D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER (writable `.data` + uninitialized `.bss`): the PE-Exec walker emits `.rdata` (read-only), `.data` (mutable initialized globals, MEM_WRITE), and `.bss` (zero-init globals, CNT_UNINITIALIZED_DATA ΓÇõ SizeOfRawData=0) from `AssembledModule.dataItems` per the item's `section`. The linker's pre-walker gate (linker.cpp) consults this set per-item. Cross-format agnosticism: this is the SAME field ELF/Mach-O exec formats declare; zero linker source changes.
$artifactProfilesComment: Profiles this format SERVES (plan 06 AP3) ΓÇõ a runnable console executable ΓçÈ cli (the format-side twin of the language's artifactProfiles[]; driver fails loud D_ArtifactProfileFormatMismatch on a profile not in this set). A `gui` profile (IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_WINDOWS_GUI) would be added when gui codegen lands ΓÇõ same format, profile-selected subsystem/entry, zero gate-code change.

pe.$comment: IMAGE_FILE_HEADER identity. machine = IMAGE_FILE_MACHINE_AMD64 (0x8664). characteristics = IMAGE_FILE_EXECUTABLE_IMAGE (0x0002) | IMAGE_FILE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE (0x0020) = 0x0022. type = exec.

optionalHeader.$comment: IMAGE_OPTIONAL_HEADER64 (PE/COFF spec ┬º3.4). magic = PE32+ (0x20B). imageBase = 0x140000000 (Windows x86_64 .exe default per MSVC). sectionAlignment = 0x1000 (4 KB virtual). fileAlignment = 0x200 (512 raw). subsystem = WINDOWS_CUI (3). dllCharacteristics = HIGH_ENTROPY_VA (0x0020) | DYNAMIC_BASE (0x0040) | NX_COMPAT (0x0100) | TERMINAL_SERVER_AWARE (0x8000) = 0x8160 (ASLR + DEP enabled). Stack/heap reserve = 1 MiB; commit = 4 KiB (MSVC defaults). OS/Subsystem versions = 6.0 (Windows Vista ΓÇõ minimum modern target).

sections.$comment: Executable code section. virtualAddress = 0x1000 ΓÇõ first page after the headers (which occupy [0, sectionAlignment) inside the ImageBase mapping). Characteristics = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_CODE (0x00000020) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_EXECUTE (0x20000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_ALIGN_16BYTES (0x00500000) = 0x60500020. Same value as the .obj schema's `.text` Characteristics (alignment + read/execute) ΓÇõ the PE32+ image just adds the RVA via the substrate `virtualAddress` field.
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only data section (D-LK2-RODATA closure). Emitted by the PE walker between .text and .idata when `AssembledModule.dataItems` carries any item with `section==Rodata`. virtualAddress=0 here is a placeholder ΓÇõ the walker computes the actual RVA at emit time as (textVirtualAddress + textVirtualSize) since sections must be contiguous in VA space. Characteristics = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_INITIALIZED_DATA (0x00000040) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) = 0x40000040 ΓÇõ initialized data, read-only (no write, no execute). The walker reads the section name + characteristics from this row; the VA is computed dynamically from layout.
sections.name: .rdata

sections.$comment: Writable initialized data (mutable globals) ΓÇõ D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER. Emitted by the PE walker after .rdata when AssembledModule.dataItems carries an item with section==Data. virtualAddress=0 is a placeholder ΓÇõ the walker computes the RVA at emit time (contiguous after .rdata). Characteristics (the substrate `type` field) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_INITIALIZED_DATA (0x00000040) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE (0x80000000) = 0xC0000040 = 3221225536 ΓÇõ initialized data, READ + WRITE (the MEM_WRITE bit is what makes a runtime store legal; a .rdata store faults). The walker reads name + characteristics from this row.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: Uninitialized data (tentative globals `int g;`) ΓÇõ D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER. Emitted by the PE walker after .data. SizeOfRawData=0 / PointerToRawData=0 (NO file footprint ΓÇõ the loader zero-fills VirtualSize bytes); contributes to SizeOfUninitializedData. virtualAddress=0 ΓÇõ walker-computed. Characteristics (`type`) = IMAGE_SCN_CNT_UNINITIALIZED_DATA (0x00000080) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ (0x40000000) | IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE (0x80000000) = 0xC0000080 = 3221225600 ΓÇõ uninitialized data, READ + WRITE.
sections.name: .bss

relocations.$comment: PE32+ image-side relocations: same kinds as the .obj schema. cycle-2 walker applies intra-module relocs in-place via the LK6 cycle 1 structured-formula triple (closes D-LK1-3-equivalent for PE). REL32 is PC-relative 32-bit; ADDR64/ADDR32 are absolute. PE32+ doesn't emit the .reloc base relocation section unless the image is rebased; ASLR-aware images that don't need rebase ship without it (cycle scope).
relocations.name: IMAGE_REL_AMD64_REL32
